behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure can come from real `git` rename detection and CLI subprocess wiring, not only local counting logic. `MOCK-MISS`: a unit parser test could pass while the CLI calls `git` without rename detection flags and never emits rename records. `REAL-INFRA`: requires real repository + real git subprocess output shape. `MOCK-HIDE`: mocked diff/name-status can hide missing/incorrect git invocation. Rejected lower level: behavior text says the tool "reads a git repository" and must classify real renames, which disqualifies Unit/Integration-only fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, assert report has the file only in `renamed` and not in `added` or `deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This is about robustness against real binary diff behavior from git subprocess output/encoding. `REAL-INFRA`: binary handling issues often appear only with real subprocess bytes and real repo content. `MOCK-HIDE`: mocked text output can miss decode/crash paths. Rejected lower level: behavior explicitly says "Diffs that include binary files" and "do not cause the tool to crash," which points to real git/process I/O boundary.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, commit and then modify/add a binary file, run CLI as subprocess, assert exit code is success and report includes binary file in the appropriate count/category.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The key failure is handling real git error/empty-history behavior (e.g., unborn HEAD) from subprocess calls. `REAL-INFRA`: depends on actual git repo state and subprocess exit/stdio. `MOCK-HIDE`: stubbing git responses can miss real command failure modes and messages. Rejected lower level: behavior text specifies "repository that has no commits yet," a real repo state boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with zero commits, run CLI via subprocess, assert successful run and empty structured report instead of exception/non-zero error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against actual git history, requiring real repository evolution plus real git command execution. `MOCK-MISS`: hand-crafted fixtures can miss mismatches caused by command options/history edge cases. `REAL-INFRA`: real subprocess and repo history are intrinsic to the claim. Rejected lower level: "match the actual change history of the repository" disqualifies pure in-process fakes.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run CLI, and assert reported counts equal counts computed from git history ground truth for that repo.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This compares behavior across real git index vs working-tree states, which is a subprocess/repo-state interaction boundary. `REAL-INFRA`: staged/unstaged semantics come from git internals, not just local function logic. `MOCK-HIDE`: mocks can force identical outputs and hide wrong command selection (`--cached` vs working tree). Rejected lower level: behavior text explicitly contrasts "staged or unstaged," requiring real git state transitions.
behaviors.test_description: Create the same logical file changes in two scenarios (unstaged and staged) in a real temp repo, run CLI for each scenario, and assert structured stdout is identical.
